trigger: git merge fails with a non-zero exit code during loop-backlog execution
prompt_template:
You are the loop-backlog orchestrator. The signal file for {task_id} is present. You are at Step 4.
The task branch is task/{task_id}.
Execute Step 4: run Bash command: git merge --no-ff task/{task_id}
This merge WILL fail (the branch does not exist or has conflicts). On failure (non-zero exit code):
  - Do NOT set status to Done.
  - Set task status to "Needs Human" using: backlog task edit {task_id} --status "Needs Human"
Start now with the git merge attempt.

context.description: When git merge fails, loop-backlog must set the task status to Needs Human (not Done, not Back to Ready). It must abort the merge cleanly and not leave the repo in a dirty merge state.

required_sequence.description: Escalate to Needs Human on merge failure

forbidden_before_step_1.description: must not set Done when merge fails
